title: Composition Graph Provider
description: Graph routes must not construct `@specd/code-graph` providers ad hoc with divergent paths or freshness rules. This spec centralizes provider creation from `SpecdConfig` so index, search, impact, and linkage handlers share one implementation and expose stale-state signals to the UI.
context: Graph routes must not construct `@specd/code-graph` providers ad hoc with divergent paths or freshness rules. This spec centralizes provider creation from `SpecdConfig` so index, search, impact, and linkage handlers share one implementation and expose stale-state signals to the UI.

rules.requirement: provider is created from project configuration
rules: The factory MUST obtain graph providers from the `createGraphProvider` function on the process-scoped `SdkHostContext` (created by `createSdkContext`). Each provider MUST be bound to the resolved `SpecdConfig` (workspaces, code roots) for the served project.

rules.requirement: indexing preparation follows the merged project-assembly model
rules:
API graph indexing MUST use SDK orchestration (`runIndexProjectGraph` from `@specd/sdk`) with the same project-level assembly semantics as the CLI:

- orchestrated workspaces from `ListWorkspaces`
- effective graph config assembled from project config
- one project-level provider index call

API composition MUST NOT reconstruct an older workspace-target contract or duplicate CLI assembly logic outside the SDK helper.
The provider (or its stats call) MUST expose freshness/stale signals consumed by `GET /v1/graph/status` so the UI can warn when the index is out of date.

Graph handlers and composition MUST obtain providers by calling `createGraphProvider` on the process-scoped `SdkHostContext`. They MUST NOT import `createCodeGraphProvider` from `@specd/code-graph` directly.

constraints: @specd/api graph composition MUST import orchestration helpers from @specd/sdk.
HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.requirement: provider is created from project configuration
scenarios.name: Provider binds to project graph paths
when: API server bootstrap creates graph provider
then: provider reads project root from config
AND index paths match workspace layout

scenarios.requirement: provider is created from project configuration
scenarios.name: Missing graph index yields empty provider state
then: AND handlers can still expose status DTO

scenarios.requirement: provider is created from project configuration
scenarios.name: Reconfigured project rebuilds provider
then: new provider instance uses updated paths
AND old in-memory graph is not reused

scenarios.requirement: indexing preparation follows the merged project-assembly model
scenarios.name: API graph indexing assembles project-level input before provider call
when: API prepares a graph index request
then: it combines ListWorkspaces output with effective graph config
AND the provider receives one project-level index input instead of legacy per-workspace targets

scenarios.name: Stale flag true when index older than sources
given: source file newer than graph index mtime

scenarios.name: Fresh index reports stale false
given: graph index rebuilt after last edit
then: AND last indexed timestamp is exposed

scenarios.name: POST index clears stale after success
Graph handler uses SdkHostContext factory
given: an API request with apiContext attached
when: a graph handler needs a provider
then: AND it does not import createCodeGraphProvider from @specd/code-graph directly
